# Tomato Varieties — 2026 Season

## San Marzano (Paste)

- **Type**: Determinate (bush), Italian heirloom
- **Days to maturity**: 78-85 days from transplant
- **Fruit**: Elongated plum shape, 4-5 oz, deep red, few seeds, thick flesh
- **Disease resistance**: Verticillium wilt (V), Fusarium wilt race 1 (F1)
- **Flavor profile**: Low acidity, sweet, meaty — ideal for sauce and canning
- **Seed source**: Seeds from Italy (ordered from Franchi Sementi via Amazon)
- **Notes**: The Lius grew San Marzano in 2025 and got approximately 15 lbs per plant. These will be the primary canning tomato. Requires sturdy staking since clusters get heavy.

## Cherokee Purple (Heirloom)

- **Type**: Indeterminate (vining), Tennessee heirloom dating to pre-1890
- **Days to maturity**: 80-90 days from transplant
- **Fruit**: Large beefsteak, 10-16 oz, dusky purple-pink with green shoulders
- **Disease resistance**: None notable — susceptible to blossom end rot and cracking
- **Flavor profile**: Rich, complex, smoky-sweet — often rated top flavor in taste tests
- **Seed source**: Baker Creek Heirloom Seeds (Mansfield, MO)
- **Notes**: Needs extra calcium supplementation to prevent blossom end rot. Kevin wants to try the "bottom watering" technique from Epic Gardening's YouTube channel to reduce fruit cracking. Requires tall indeterminate cages (6 ft minimum).

## Sungold (Cherry)

- **Type**: Indeterminate, F1 hybrid
- **Days to maturity**: 55-65 days from transplant (earliest variety in the garden)
- **Fruit**: Small cherry, 0.5-1 oz, bright orange, thin skin
- **Disease resistance**: Fusarium wilt (F1, F2), Tobacco Mosaic Virus (TMV)
- **Flavor profile**: Exceptionally sweet (Brix 9-10), tropical undertones, bursts when bitten
- **Seed source**: Johnny's Selected Seeds (Winslow, ME)
- **Notes**: Incredibly productive — expect 500+ fruits per plant. Will be planted in succession (two plants April 15, two plants May 1) for extended harvest. Tends to sprawl; needs aggressive pruning of suckers.

## Early Girl (Slicer)

- **Type**: Indeterminate, F1 hybrid
- **Days to maturity**: 50-62 days from transplant (earliest slicer)
- **Fruit**: Medium globe, 4-6 oz, classic red
- **Disease resistance**: Verticillium (V), Fusarium (F1, F2)
- **Flavor profile**: Well-balanced acidity and sweetness, good all-purpose slicer
- **Seed source**: Territorial Seed Company (Cottage Grove, OR — local!)
- **Notes**: Chosen specifically for Portland's shorter warm season. Will produce ripe fruit by early July even in cool summers. Sarah plans to use green tomatoes from late-season harvests for fried green tomatoes and green tomato chutney.
